title: Chromosomal inheritance
description: Test your knowledge of chromosomal inheritance!

assessment_items.question_content:
The diagram below shows two pairs of homologous chromosomes in a diploid cell. The larger chromosomes have different alleles of Gene *A*, and the smaller chromosomes have different alleles of Gene *B*.

[[☃ image 1]]

**If the cell undergoes meiosis, which of the following is true regarding the allele combinations in the resulting gametes?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints: During meiosis I, pairs of homologous chromosomes align randomly along the metaphase plate.

So, for two pairs of homologous chromosomes in a cell, there are two ways the pairs can line up relative to one another during metaphase I:

[[☃ image 1]]

According to the diagram, there are four possible allele combinations in the resulting gametes: *ab*, *AB*, *Ab*, and *aB*. In addition, the diagram shows us that each combination is equally likely to occur.
So, allele combination *AB* is just as likely to occur in a gamete as allele combination *Ab* is.

options.alt: A circle represents a cell. The cell contains the following chromosomes: large chromosome labeled lowercase a (maternally inherited), large chromosome labeled uppercase A (paternally inherited), small chromosome labeled lowercase b (maternally inherited), and small chromosome labeled uppercase B (paternally inherited). The alleles for uppercase and lowercase A are depicted as red and the alleles for uppercase and lowercase B are depicted as orange.

choices.content: Allele combination *AB* is just as likely to occur in a gamete as allele combination *Ab* is.
Allele combination *Aa* is just as likely to occur in a gamete as allele combination *aB* is.
Allele combination *ab* is less likely to occur in a gamete than allele combination *AB* is.
Allele combination *Bb* is more likely to occur in a gamete than allele combination *Ab* is.

assessment_items.question_content:
The diagram below shows two pairs of homologous chromosomes in a diploid cell. The larger chromosomes have different alleles of Gene *H*, and the smaller chromosomes have different alleles of Gene *G*.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following statements is true regarding the gametes formed when cells like this one undergo meiosis?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints: During meiosis I, pairs of homologous chromosomes align randomly along the metaphase plate.

So, for two pairs of homologous chromosomes in a cell, there are two ways the pairs can line up relative to one another during metaphase I:

[[☃ image 1]]

According to the diagram, this process can produce four types of gametes, each with a unique combination of alleles (either *GH*, *gh*, *Gh*, or *gH*). In addition, the diagram shows us that these different gamete types occur with equal frequency.
So, gametes with alleles *G* and *H* occur at the same frequency as gametes with alleles *g* and *H*.

options.alt: A circle represents a cell. The cell contains the following chromosomes: large chromosome labeled uppercase H (maternally inherited), large chromosome labeled lowercase h (paternally inherited), small chromosome labeled uppercase G (maternally inherited), and small chromosome labeled lowercase g (paternally inherited).  The alleles for uppercase and lowercase G are depicted as blue and the alleles for uppercase and lowercase H are depicted as green.

choices.content: Gametes with alleles *G* and *H* occur at the same frequency as gametes with alleles *g* and *H*.
Gametes with alleles *G* and *g* occur at the same frequency as gametes with alleles *G* and *H*.
Gametes with alleles *G* and *H* occur at a lower frequency than gametes with alleles *g* and *h*.
Gametes with alleles *H* and *h* occur at a higher frequency than gametes with alleles *G* and *h*.

assessment_items.question_content:
The diagram below shows two pairs of homologous chromosomes in a diploid cell. The larger chromosomes have different alleles of Gene *Q*, and the smaller chromosomes have different alleles of Gene *R*.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following best represents the types and frequencies of gametes formed when cells like this one undergo meiosis?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints: During meiosis I, pairs of homologous chromosomes align randomly along the metaphase plate.

So, for two pairs of homologous chromosomes in a cell, there are two ways the pairs can line up relative to one another during metaphase I:

[[☃ image 1]]

According to the diagram, there are four possible allele combinations in the resulting gametes: *rq*, *RQ*, *rQ*, and *Rq*. In addition, the diagram shows us that each combination occurs with a frequency of about $25\%$.

So, the correct answer is

[[☃ image 1]]

options.alt: A circle represents a cell. The cell contains the following chromosomes: large chromosome labeled lowercase q (maternally inherited), large chromosome labeled uppercase Q (paternally inherited), small chromosome labeled lowercase r (maternally inherited), and small chromosome labeled uppercase R (paternally inherited). The alleles for uppercase and lowercase Q are depicted as orange and the alleles for uppercase and lowercase R are depicted as blue.

choices.content: ![Four circles, each containing 2 chromosomes. Circle 1, labeled 25%, contains small maternal chromosome labeled lowercase r and large maternal chromosome labeled lowercase q. Circle 2, labeled 25%, contains small paternal chromosome labeled uppercase R and large paternal chromosome labeled uppercase Q. Circle 3, labeled 25%, contains small maternal chromosome labeled lowercase r and large paternal chromosome labeled uppercase Q. Circle 4, labeled 25%, contains small paternal chromosome labeled uppercase R and large maternal chromosome labeled lowercase q. The alleles for uppercase and lowercase Q are depicted as orange and the alleles for uppercase and lowercase R are depicted as blue.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0e10b8c0864022af8e0faead963e43c3.png)

![Four circles, each containing 2 chromosomes. Circle 1, labeled 35%, contains small maternal chromosome labeled lowercase r and large maternal chromosome labeled lowercase q. Circle 2, labeled 35%, contains small paternal chromosome labeled uppercase R and large paternal chromosome labeled uppercase Q. Circle 3, labeled 15%, contains small maternal chromosome labeled lowercase r and large paternal chromosome labeled uppercase Q. Circle 4, labeled 15%, contains small paternal chromosome labeled uppercase R and large maternal chromosome labeled lowercase q. The alleles for uppercase and lowercase Q are depicted as orange and the alleles for uppercase and lowercase R are depicted as blue.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6c71fa9cd8f9f60083134efd85f044cd.png)

![Four circles, each containing 2 chromosomes. Circle 1, labeled 25%, contains large maternal chromosome labeled lowercase q and large maternal chromosome labeled lowercase q. Circle 2, labeled 25%, contains large paternal chromosome labeled uppercase Q and large paternal chromosome labeled uppercase Q. Circle 3, labeled 25%, contains small maternal chromosome labeled lowercase r and small maternal chromosome labeled lowercase r. Circle 4, labeled 25%, contains small paternal chromosome labeled uppercase R and small paternal chromosome labeled uppercase r. The alleles for uppercase and lowercase Q are depicted as orange and the alleles for uppercase and lowercase R are depicted as blue.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/964a74c097e8b18966dd15270687c738.png)

Circle 3, labeled 15%, contains large paternal chromosome labeled uppercase Q and large maternal chromosome labeled lowercase q. assessment_items.question_content:
The following diagram depicts two diploid cells, one from a male and one from a female of the same species. In each cell, two pairs of homologous chromosomes are shown. The larger chromosomes have different alleles of Gene *A*, and the smaller chromosomes have different alleles of Gene *B*.

[[☃ image 1]]

**If the male and female mate, which of the following will be true about their offspring?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints: To answer this question, we’ll need to compare the genotypes of the offspring with those of their parents. First, let’s determine the parental genotypes: According to the diagram, the genotype of the male parent is *AaBb* and the genotype of the female parent is *aabb*.

Next, let’s determine the genotypes of the offspring produced by these two parents. The male parent produces four types of gametes (*AB*, *Ab*, *aB*, and *ab*), while the female parent produces only one type of gamete (*ab*). We can diagram a cross between these two individuals as follows:

[[☃ image 1]]

Of the four offspring genotypes in this diagram, two correspond to parental genotypes and two are new genotypes.
So, roughly half of the offspring will have a different genotype than either parent.

options.alt: Two circles represent a male and a female cell. The male cell contains the following chromosomes: large chromosome labeled uppercase A, large chromosome labeled lowercase a, small chromosome labeled uppercase B, and small chromosome labeled lowercase b. The female cell contains the following chromosomes: large chromosome labeled lowercase a, large chromosome labeled lowercase a, small chromosome labeled lowercase b, and small chromosome labeled lowercase b. The alleles for uppercase and lowercase A are depicted as red and the alleles for uppercase and lowercase B are depicted as orange.

choices.content: All of the offspring will have a different genotype than either parent.
Roughly half of the offspring will have a different genotype than either parent.
None of the offspring will have the same genotype as the male parent.
Roughly half of the offspring will have the same genotype as the female parent.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following diagram depicts two diploid cells, one from a male and one from a female of the same species. In each cell, two pairs of homologous chromosomes are shown. The larger chromosomes have different alleles of Gene *D*, and the smaller chromosomes have different alleles of Gene *E*.

[[☃ image 1]]

**If the male and female mate, which of the following will be true about their offspring?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints: To answer this question, we’ll need to compare the genotypes of the offspring with those of their parents. First, let’s determine the parental genotypes: According to the diagram, the genotype of the male parent is *DdEE* and the genotype of the female parent is *Ddee*.

Next, let’s determine the genotypes of the offspring produced by these two parents. The male parent produces two types of gametes (*DE* and *dE*), and the female parent also produces two types of gametes (*De* and *de*). We can diagram a cross between these two individuals as follows:

[[☃ image 1]]

According to the diagram, all of the offspring genotypes correspond to new, non-parental genotypes.
So, none of the offspring will have a parental genotype.

options.alt: Two circles represent a male and a female cell. The male cell contains the following chromosomes: large chromosome labeled uppercase D, large chromosome labeled lowercase d, small chromosome labeled uppercase E, and small chromosome labeled uppercase E. The female cell contains the following chromosomes: large chromosome labeled uppercase D, large chromosome labeled lowercase d, small chromosome labeled lowercase e, and small chromosome labeled lowercase e. The alleles for uppercase and lowercase D are depicted as light blue and the alleles for uppercase and lowercase E are depicted as orange.

choices.content: All of the offspring will have a parental genotype.
None of the offspring will have a parental genotype.
Roughly half of the offspring will have the genotype of the female parent.
Roughly a quarter of the offspring will have the genotype of the male parent.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following diagram depicts two diploid cells, one from a male and one from a female of the same species. In each cell, two pairs of homologous chromosomes are shown. The larger chromosomes have different alleles of Gene *F*, and the smaller chromosomes have different alleles of Gene *G*.

[[☃ image 1]]

**If the male and female mate, which of the following will be true about their offspring?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints: To answer this question, we’ll need to compare the genotypes of the offspring with those of their parents. First, let’s determine the parental genotypes: According to the diagram, the genotype of the male parent is *Ffgg* and the genotype of the female parent is *ffGg*.

Next, let’s determine the genotypes of the offspring produced by these two parents. The male parent produces two types of gametes (*Fg* and *fg*), and the female parent also produces two types of gamete (*fG* and *fg*). We can diagram a cross between these two individuals as follows: 

[[☃ image 1]]

Of the four offspring genotypes in this diagram, two correspond to parental genotypes and two are new, non-parental genotypes.
So, roughly half of the offspring will have a non-parental genotype.

options.alt: Two circles represent a male and a female cell. The male cell contains the following chromosomes: large chromosome labeled uppercase F, large chromosome labeled lowercase f, small chromosome labeled lowercase g, and small chromosome labeled lowercase g. The female cell contains the following chromosomes: large chromosome labeled lowercase f, large chromosome labeled lowercase f, small chromosome labeled uppercase G, and small chromosome labeled lowercase g. The alleles for uppercase and lowercase F are depicted as pink and the alleles for uppercase and lowercase G are depicted as blue.

choices.content: None of the offspring will have the genotype of the male parent.
Roughly half of the offspring will have the genotype of the female parent.
All of the offspring will have a non-parental genotype.
Roughly half of the offspring will have a non-parental genotype.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following diagram shows a karyotype of a certain human.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Based on the karyotype, which of the following statements is most likely true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.alt: A karyotype with 23 pairs of chromosomes. Chromosome pair 1 is the largest and the pairs get increasingly smaller through pair 22. Chromosome pair 21 actually contains 3 chromosomes. Chromosome pair 23 is labeled uppercase X uppercase Y and contains a long and a short chromosome, while all other chromosome pairs contain identical chromosomes.

choices.content: The individual has a genetic condition caused by the nondisjunction of chromosomes during meiosis.
The individual has a genetic condition caused by having X and Y chromosomes of different sizes.
The individual has a genetic condition caused by a single nucleotide substitution on chromosome 3.
The individual has a genetic condition caused by an autosomal duplication that occurred during gamete formation.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following diagram shows a karyotype of a certain human.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Based on the karyotype, which of the following statements is most likely true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.alt: A karyotype with 23 pairs of chromosomes. Chromosome pair 1 is the largest and pairs get increasing smaller through pair 22. Chromosome pair 23 is labeled uppercase X uppercase Y and contains a long and a short chromosome and chromosome pair 1 contains one chromosome that is slightly shorter than the other. . All other chromosome pairs contain identical chromosomes.

choices.content: The individual has a genetic condition caused by a chromosomal duplication.
The individual has a genetic condition caused by the X and Y chromosomes being different sizes.
The individual has a genetic condition caused by a nondisjunction event.
The individual has a genetic condition caused by a single nucleotide substitution on chromosome 16.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following diagram shows a karyotype of a certain human.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Based on the karyotype, which of the following statements is most likely true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.alt: A karyotype with 23 pairs of chromosomes. Chromosome pair 1 is the largest and the pairs get increasing smaller through pair 22. Chromosome pair 10 contains a long and a short chromosome, while all other chromosome pairs contain identical chromosomes. Chromosome pair 23 is labeled uppercase X uppercase X and is similar in size to pair 5.

choices.content: The individual has a genetic condition caused by a chromosomal deletion.
The individual has a genetic condition caused by chromosomes 1 and 2 being different sizes.
The individual has a genetic condition caused by a nondisjunction event.
The individual has a genetic condition caused by a single nucleotide substitution on chromosome 7.

assessment_items.question_content:
The diagram below shows a nondisjunction event for a single human chromosome during gamete formation. The chromosome depicted in the diagram contains Gene *A*.

[[☃ image 1]]

**If Gamete 2 is fertilized, how will the nondisjunction event affect the resulting embryo?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.alt: A circle contains a pair of joined chromosomes, each with a microtubule attached and a blue band near the bottom labeled Gene A. The circle splits into two circles. The first circle, labeled Gamete 1, is empty. The second circle, labeled Gamete 2, contains 2 individual chromosomes, each with a blue band.

choices.content: The embryo will have higher-than-normal levels of the Gene *A* protein product in its tissues.
The embryo will not be viable because monosomy is not typically tolerated in humans.
The embryo will have four copies of Gene *A* in each cell.
The embryo will not be able to produce the Gene *A* protein product.

assessment_items.question_content:
The diagram below shows a chromosomal rearrangement in a human cell during gamete formation. The chromosomes in the diagram contain Gene *P*.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What will be the most likely effect of the chromosomal rearrangement on an embryo formed by the fertilization of Gamete 3?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.alt: A circle contains 2 identical joined pairs of chromosomes, each with a microtubule attached to its center. Each chromosome has a band near the bottom labeled gene uppercase P. Metaphase 1 occurs and the circle splits into 2 circles. The first circle contains a joined pair of chromosomes, each with a microtubule attached to its center. The left chromosome has 1 band, while the right chromosome is shorter and does not have a band. The second circle contains a joined pair of chromosomes, each with a microtubule attached to its center. The left chromosome is longer and has 2 bands, while the right chromosome has 1 band. Metaphase 2 occurs and each circle further splits into 2 circles. Each of the new circles contains 1 individual chromosome. The first circle produces gamete 1, containing a chromosome with 1 band, and gamete 2, containing a short chromosome with no band. The second circle produces gamete 3, containing a long chromosome with 2 bands, and gamete 4, containing a chromosome with 1 band.

choices.content: The embryo will have higher-than-normal levels of the Gene *P* protein product in its tissues.
The embryo will have four copies of Gene *P* in each cell.
The embryo will have two copies of Gene *P* in each cell.
The embryo will have lower-than-normal levels of the Gene *P* protein product in its tissues.

assessment_items.question_content:
The diagram below shows a chromosomal rearrangement in a human cell during gamete formation. The chromosomes in the diagram contain Gene *H*.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What will be the most likely effect of the chromosomal rearrangement on an embryo formed by the fertilization of Gamete 2?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.alt: A circle contains 2 identical joined pairs of chromosomes, each with a microtubule attached to its center. Each chromosome has a band near the bottom labeled gene uppercase H. Metaphase 1 occurs and the circle splits into 2 circles. The first circle contains a joined pair of chromosomes, each with a microtubule attached to its center. The left chromosome has 1 band, while the right chromosome is shorter and does not have a band. The second circle contains a joined pair of chromosomes, each with a microtubule attached to its center. The left chromosome is longer and has 2 bands, while the right chromosome has 1 band. Metaphase 2 occurs and each circle further splits into 2 circles. Each of the new circles contains 1 individual chromosome. The first circle produces gamete 1, containing a chromosome with 1 band, and gamete 2, containing a short chromosome with no band. The second circle produces gamete 3, containing a long chromosome with 2 bands, and gamete 4, containing a chromosome with 1 band.

choices.content: The embryo will have lower-than-normal levels of the Gene *H* protein product in its cells.
The embryo will have higher-than-normal levels of the Gene *H* protein product in its cells.
The embryo will have two copies of Gene *H* in each cell.
The embryo will not have any copies of Gene *H* in its cells.
